book_summary: Leviticus has chapters 1-22 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The opening offering manual is framed as ancient ritual grammar for drawing near after the tabernacle is filled with glory. Chapters 8-10 narrate priestly ordination, glory, rupture, grief, and discernment. Chapters 11-15 cover clean/unclean animals, childbirth purification, scale-disease/tzaraat diagnosis and cleansing, garment/house affliction, and bodily discharges with strong anti-shame and body-honoring safeguards. Chapter 16 gives the Day of Atonement as sanctuary cleansing and communal burden-removal. Chapters 17-20 open holiness-code material around blood/life, sanctuary-centered slaughter, sexual-boundary texts, neighbor and sojourner love, disability justice, wages, honest measures, child-sacrifice polemic, and severe penalty texts. Chapters 21-22 preserve priestly holiness, holy food, and offering-acceptability boundaries with explicit disability-justice, anti-misogyny, anti-violence, and anti-shame safeguards.

translation_notes: Leviticus continues Exodus: YHWH calls from the completed tent of meeting.
Korban/offering means something brought near; offerings are approach/nearness grammar, not crude appeasement.
Blood/life should be treated as ritual life-symbolism, not gore spectacle.
Clean/unclean language is ritual-state language, not body shame, moral disgust, disability stigma, or human worth ranking.
Bodies, flows, birth, illness, sex, and menstruation are not evil; washing, waiting, and offerings protect sanctuary boundaries without degrading bodies.

Leviticus 18 and 20 sexual-boundary texts are source-preserved and require deep lexical, historical, and reception-history audit. They must not be used to justify hatred, violence, bullying, criminalization, or dehumanization of LGBTQ people.
Leviticus 19 is a central holiness/social ethics chapter: field margins, wages, disability justice, truth, courts, neighbor love, sojourner love, elder honor, and honest measures.
Leviticus 21 priestly corpse-contact and mourning restrictions are role-specific holiness boundaries, not general contempt for grief or family love.
Leviticus 21 bodily-wholeness restrictions are ancient priestly altar-boundary rules and must not be interpreted as reduced human worth, disability shame, or Divine dislike of disabled bodies.
Leviticus 21:22 is crucial: priests with bodily blemish may eat holy food; exclusion is from altar/veil service, not from covenant belonging or holy provision.
Leviticus 22 offering blemish restrictions concern ritual acceptability of sacrificial animals, not a theology that disabled bodies are inferior.
Leviticus 22 holy-food rules resolve temporary impurity by washing and sunset; return is built into the system.
Leviticus 22 ends with Exodus liberation memory: the Sanctifier is the Liberator from Egypt.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Leviticus/Vayiqra title and opening call from tent requires Torah continuity crosslink with Exodus 40.
Clean/unclean terminology requires anti-shame and ritual-state metadata across Leviticus.
Leviticus 18:22 and 20:13 require dedicated lexical/context/reception audit with anti-queer-harm safeguards.
Leviticus 18/20 land-defilement and land-vomit rhetoric requires anti-colonial and anti-violence interpretation safeguards.
Leviticus 19:14 disability justice language requires anti-ableism metadata.
Leviticus 19:20 enslaved/unfree woman case requires coercion, consent, and anti-slavery audit.
Leviticus 20 severe penalty material requires ancient legal-context and anti-weaponization audit.
Leviticus 21:9 priest daughter penalty material requires anti-misogyny and anti-violence audit.
Leviticus 21 bodily blemish restrictions require disability-justice and priestly-ritual-boundary audit.
Leviticus 22 priestly holy-food household eligibility requires gender, household, and anti-slavery audit.
Leviticus 22 animal blemish offering restrictions require ritual acceptability vs disability-theology distinction.
Leviticus 22 mother-and-young rule requires animal-life boundary audit.
next_actions: Continue Leviticus with chapter 23: appointed times/festivals, Sabbath, Passover/Unleavened Bread, firstfruits, Weeks, trumpet memorial, Day of Atonement, Booths, and harvest justice insertion.
